findings.implication: Applicant CV: Lists BSc Pharmacy and roles as Research Assistant/Clinical Coordinator, demonstrating relevant research experience and training.
Applicant CV: Lists two peer-reviewed publications in Movement Disorders and Journal of Parkinson's Disease, supporting impactful research outputs.
Applicant CV: Shows progression from Research Assistant to Clinical Coordinator, indicating increasing responsibility in research roles.
Applicant CV: Includes a role as Clinical Coordinator at Barts Health NHS Trust alongside academic research, supporting practitioner-academic career commitment.
Applicant CV: Publications on Parkinson's Disease and Diabetes suggest prior research quality and relevance to potential health-focused proposals.
Applicant Research Background: Clearly states a career vision to become a mixed methods researcher and outlines a trajectory from Pre-doctoral to Doctoral Fellowship.
Applicant Research Background: Explains the award is the right next step to gain theoretical underpinnings for a future PhD, building on existing hands-on experience.
Applicant Research Background: Details specific mixed methods experience (surveys, interviews, thematic analysis, regression) and formal training in statistics and research methods.
Applicant Research Background: Mentions securing small grants (£500, £700) and leading community engagement activities, demonstrating practical research outputs and impact.
Applicant Research Background: Shows increasing responsibility from volunteer researcher to lead applicant for grants and Clinical Coordinator managing PPIE.
Applicant Research Background: Highlights role as Clinical Coordinator at Barts Health NHS Trust, providing insights into care pathways and balancing clinical/research duties.
Applicant Research Background: Describes prior successful projects (dissertation, ELPD, Mapping Practices) demonstrating quality and relevance of previous research.
Applicant Research Background: Explicitly details work with under-served communities (British Bangladeshi, East London) and co-developing culturally appropriate protocols.
Applicant Research Background: Describes PPIE activities and co-production across project stages, ensuring research aligns with participant priorities.
